name: Oats

disambiguationNotes: Canonical FoodRanked oats means uncooked rolled oats.
Do not substitute steel-cut oats, oat groats, instant oats, or cooked oatmeal values.

Current USDA anchor is the closest acceptable plain-oats row in hand, but future upgrades should prefer an equally trustworthy rolled-oats-specific canonical row if one materially improves identity fit.

secondarySources.note: Exact FDC row identified in this pass. It remains the current best available primary anchor in hand, but it is still weaker than a rolled-oats-specific commodity record and should be upgraded later if a clearly better trustworthy match is adopted.

changeNotes: Production draft upgraded with explicit USDA anchor fdcId 2708489 and foodCode 57602100.
Project canonical identity is now locked to uncooked rolled oats for future FoodRanked entries.
Current source row is retained as the best available plain-oats primary row in hand for the production draft, with the identity caveat documented rather than hidden.
Future source improvements should prefer a clearly rolled-oats-specific canonical row without changing the user-facing identity of this entry.

protein_g.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
protein_g.note: Canonical anchor row for protein on the currently attached plain-oats source row. Current file value 13.2 is aligned to the attached source row, which is being used as the best currently attached primary source for the rolled-oats canonical entry.

kcal.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
kcal.note: Canonical anchor row for energy on the currently attached plain-oats source row. Current file value 379 is aligned to the attached source row, which is being used as the best currently attached primary source for the rolled-oats canonical entry.

fibre_g.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
fibre_g.note: Canonical anchor row for fibre. Updated to the attached USDA value 10.1 g for the current generic raw-oats identity.

omega3_mg.sourceName: USDA FoodData Central API food details
omega3_mg.note: Adjusted to a conservative ~100 mg total omega-3-equivalent from the attached raw-oats fatty-acid rows, replacing the earlier loose 111 mg placeholder.

essential_amino_acids_score.note: Displayed as N/A for now. No defensible reusable amino-acid scoring method has been attached for this exact food identity.

nonessential_amino_acids_score.note: Displayed as N/A for now. No defensible reusable amino-acid scoring method has been attached for this exact food identity.

glycemic_index.note: GI for oats remains unresolved because GI is strongly form-sensitive and the currently attached primary row is still only an approximate identity fit for rolled oats. Keep displayed as draft-grade until a form-specific GI source is attached.

bioavailability_percent.note: Displayed as N/A for now. This metric remains part of the final display schema, but the project does not yet have a defensible sourced method for the canonical rolled-oats entry.

pros.title: beta-glucans are a real category-level advantage
pros.explanation: Oats are one of the clearest staple grains for soluble fibre, especially beta-glucans, so the upside is more specific than generic whole-grain praise.

evidence.sourceName: established oats/beta-glucan literature; exact citation still to be attached
evidence.citationNote: Strongly supported claim, but this file still needs the exact citation bundle used in production.

pros.title: contain avenanthramides that most grains do not
pros.explanation: Avenanthramides are one of the more distinctive oat-specific phytochemical talking points, which helps oats avoid generic grain filler.

evidence.sourceName: oat phytochemical literature; exact citation still to be attached
evidence.citationNote: Directionally strong claim, but exact review citation still missing from the file.

pros.title: easy to build into filling meals
pros.explanation: This is a practical-food upside, but it is partly editorial and should not be over-weighted as hard science.

evidence.citationNote: Useful for narration context, not a strong scientific differentiator.

cons.title: phytates can modestly complicate mineral absorption
cons.explanation: That does not erase the mineral value of oats, but it is a fair qualifier when talking about mineral strength.

evidence.sourceName: grain phytate / mineral absorption literature; exact citation still to be attached
evidence.citationNote: Reasonable caveat with literature support, but exact production citation still missing.

cons.title: processing level can materially change the glycemic story
cons.explanation: Instant oats and finer oat products can behave quite differently from plain rolled oats, which is exactly why this file needs identity lock.

evidence.sourceName: GI / oat processing literature; exact citation still to be attached
evidence.citationNote: Strongly plausible and central to why this draft exists, but exact citation is still pending.

cons.title: common sweet serving patterns can hide the plain food's quality
cons.explanation: This is about real-world usage, not the raw ingredient itself, so it belongs as editorial context rather than scientific scoring evidence.

evidence.citationNote: Keep for narration if wanted; do not pretend this is a nutrient-source claim.

primaryRemainingBlockers: Attach a form-specific GI citation for uncooked rolled oats.
Upgrade the primary source row later if a clearly better rolled-oats-specific trustworthy row is adopted.
Keep all future oats variants as separate entries rather than drifting this canonical rolled-oats entry.

sourceNotes: Draft production-lane entry upgraded with USDA anchor fdcId 2708489 / foodCode 57602100.
Canonical FoodRanked oats is now locked to uncooked rolled oats.
Current numeric panel is aligned to the best currently attached plain-oats primary row in hand, with the remaining identity caveat documented explicitly instead of silently drifting the entry.
GI remains unresolved until a form-specific rolled-oats GI source is attached.
